basics.name: António Pereira
basics.label: OutSystems Architect & Champion
basics.summary: Senior OutSystems Solution Architect with a decade of experience designing, delivering, and scaling enterprise applications. OutSystems Champion since 2022 and Architecture Specialist (O11 + ODC) since 2022 — with 3+ years of formal architecture practice leadership across KinetIT and Babel (Top OutSystems Partner Worldwide). Career spans R&D engineering at OutSystems, delivery management for enterprise consultancies, and current Solution Architect role at Babel.

work.name: Babel
work.summary: Architected OutSystems solutions for the Portuguese securities/capital-markets regulator, ensuring compliance with supervisory and reporting requirements over regulated entities.
highlights: Defined OutSystems solution architecture for CMVM projects, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements for capital markets and securities oversight.
Designed application structure and data model supporting supervision, registration, reporting, and inspection processes over regulated entities.
Ran technical audits — performance, scalability, security, technical debt — and produced recommendation reports for in-flight projects.
Validated architecture and design decisions against OutSystems best practices (Architecture Canvas, 4-Layer Canvas) and CMVM's internal standards.
Supported delivery teams on complex technical challenges — legacy-system integrations, large-data processing, regulatory business-rule implementation.

work.name: Babel
work.summary: Architectural leadership across Babel's OutSystems practice in parallel with named client engagements — cross-account audits, pre-sales architecture, mentoring, and contribution to firm-wide platform recognition.
highlights: Helped position Babel as a Top OutSystems Partner Worldwide and the
Provided architectural guidance and audits across multiple parallel client engagements, applying OutSystems best practices (Architecture Canvas, 4-Layer Canvas) consistently across the portfolio.
Served as the technical voice for inbound OutSystems opportunities — pre-sales scoping, technical proposal defence, and POC delivery.
Mentored delivery teams across business-critical accounts, partnering with engineers to unblock complex architectural challenges and raise the bar on performance and quality.
Represented Babel's OutSystems practice in the community as an OutSystems Champion — sustained Forge contributions, partner-network engagement, and community presence.

work.name: Babel
work.summary: Defined frontend architecture for the IRN registry support modules; piloted and rolled out Claude AI as a development accelerator across the frontend team.
highlights: Defined frontend architecture, component patterns, navigation structure and UI guidelines for IRN's registry support modules; mentored the frontend team on architectural alignment and usability requirements.

Piloted and rolled out Claude AI as a frontend development accelerator — concrete use cases (code generation, component prototyping, code review, unit-test creation, technical docs) and AI-assisted workflows with prompting best practices and output-validation gates.
Drove team adoption of Claude AI through training sessions, knowledge sharing, and a reusable prompt library tailored to the IRN context.
Ensured integration with IRN's existing civil registry backend services and APIs; enforced compliance with security, RGPD, and Portuguese public-sector standards.
Monitored productivity and quality metrics before/after AI introduction, iterating to maximize AI's impact on the development cycle.

work.name: Babel
work.summary: Architected an OutSystems platform for NOS employee benefits — multi-benefit catalog, self-service portal, configurable eligibility rules, and integration with NOS core HR systems.
highlights: Architected an OutSystems platform for employee benefits (health, education, well-being, mobility, insurance, partnerships, incentives) with eligibility rules configurable by role, seniority, level and contract type.
Designed the employee self-service portal with submission, tracking, plafond visibility, history, and the submit/validate/approve workflow with auto-routing by benefit type and approval hierarchy.
Led the integration strategy with NOS core systems (SAP HR, Active Directory) for employee, hierarchy, and organizational-structure sync.

Designed HR management dashboards (adoption per benefit, plafond consumption, cost trends, satisfaction) and a role-based access model (employee, manager, HR, admin, auditor) with encryption of personal/financial data.

Supervised delivery teams technically — architecture and code reviews, OutSystems best-practices enforcement (Architecture Canvas, 4-Layer Canvas), and a dynamic-config model for adding new benefits without code changes.

work.name: Babel
work.summary: Solution Architect and Team Leader on a nationally-critical Portuguese government program — re-platforming IRN's request submission and processing onto .NET Core microservices with a React/Next.js frontend.
highlights: Acted as Solution Architect and Team Leader for a nationally-critical Portuguese government project — unified platform for IRN request submission and processing, with full tech redesign and database remodeling.

Led a multidisciplinary Scrum team of 10 inside a wider program of 70+ people across business and technology streams; served as the bridge between business and technology, aligning technical solutions with business needs.
Drove the re-platform from OutSystems to .NET Core / microservices / containers with a Next.js / React / TypeScript frontend and ApiSix API Gateway for performance, security and scalability.
Built the CI/CD foundation with Jenkins, ArgoCD, Sonatype Nexus Repository, and GitLab.
Operated in a context of business and technical modernization, leading prioritization, stakeholder negotiation, and architecture decisions across the program.

work.name: KinetIT
work.summary: Led the OutSystems practice — trained developers, owned architectural reviews and estimations, and built KinetIT's OutSystems Knowledge Base.
highlights: Helped drive the KinetIT OutSystems practice to a 5.0 average customer rating and the OutSystems Quality App badge.
Led the OutSystems practice — trained and coached developers through certifications, kept the team current on platform releases, and grew the OutSystems skill base inside the org.
Built the OutSystems Knowledge Base — a centralized repository of patterns, code samples, and docs that cut ramp-up time for new projects.
Owned architectural reviews, estimations, and POCs across the practice; the technical voice for new opportunities.
Delivered projects end-to-end against budget and timeline while keeping stakeholders aligned.

work.name: KinetIT
work.summary: Architected an OutSystems platform for managing and analyzing wind-tunnel aerodynamic tests across Volkswagen Group brands, including real-time telemetry ingestion from SCADA and data-logger systems.
highlights: Designed an OutSystems-based solution for managing and analyzing wind-tunnel aerodynamic tests for Volkswagen Group brands — VW, Audi, Porsche, SEAT, Škoda, Lamborghini, Bentley.
Architected the data model and application structure for multi-brand configurations (brand, model, component type) with telemetry processing at scale.
Designed the test-configuration module with flexible parametrization of aerodynamic variables (wind speed, attack angle, temperature, pressure, drag and lift coefficients).

Architected real-time data ingestion from wind-tunnel acquisition systems (sensors, data loggers, SCADA) and the analysis layer with interactive dashboards comparing results across versions and brands.

Defined role-based access (test engineer, tunnel manager, data analyst, brand lead) and IP/sensitive-data protection across the group; supervised delivery teams and validated scalability for new tunnels, brands, and test types.

work.name: KinetIT
work.summary: Managed OutSystems delivery teams as both technical lead and people manager; embedded with partner consultancies on critical engagements.
highlights: Managed OutSystems delivery teams as both technical lead and people manager.
Embedded with Do iT Lean for a single OutSystems delivery project during this period.

work.name: KinetIT
work.summary: Tech-led an automated invoice-collection platform for the Luz Saúde healthcare group, with bidirectional Salesforce CRM integration, multi-channel communications, and risk-based debtor segmentation.
highlights: Tech-led the OutSystems platform for automated identification and management of unpaid-invoice collection processes at Luz Saúde, the major Portuguese healthcare group.
Designed the configurable rule engine (deadlines, late-stage tiers, contact strategies) and the staged auto-collection workflow (friendly reminder → formal notice → pre-litigation → litigation).
Architected bidirectional Salesforce CRM integration — case/task creation, client and opportunity sync, REST-API consultation — giving commercial teams real-time collection-status visibility.

Built multi-channel automated communications (email/SMS/letter with stage-and-segment templates), payment-agreement management, debtor segmentation by risk/amount/history, and dashboards (debt aging, recovery rate, contact-channel efficacy).
Ensured RGPD compliance for personal/financial data, role-based access (collection manager, finance analyst, commercial, admin), audit trail for legal/compliance, and reporting for regulators.

work.name: Outfit (now Devoteam)
work.summary: OutSystems Tech Lead across consulting engagements — owned product architecture, code quality, and team mentoring across client projects.
highlights: Tech-led a from-scratch OutSystems recruitment management product for Bold, plus an offline-capable branch front-office banking solution for EuroBic (engagement via KPMG).
Defined application architecture for scalability and performance; enforced OutSystems standards (Architecture Canvas, 4-Layer Canvas) across both engagements.
Mentored developers, owned estimation and delivery planning, and partnered with UX/UI, QA, DevOps and client business teams.

work.name: OutSystems
work.summary: Built features inside Service Studio — the IDE used by every OutSystems developer — owning developer experience for frontend app building.
highlights: Built features inside Service Studio — the IDE used by every OutSystems developer.
Owned the developer experience for frontend application building on the platform.

work.name: OutSystems
work.summary: User advocate inside R&D — steered platform design toward simplicity, consistency, and learnability; wrote and maintained product documentation.
highlights: User advocate inside R&D — steered platform design toward simplicity, consistency, and learnability.
Wrote and maintained product copy, how-tos, reference docs, and API documentation.
Facilitated user tests to validate new feature UX before they shipped.

education.summary: Major in Multimedia Systems, Minor in Enterprise Information Systems. Grant holder at DEI.

awards.title: OutSystems Champion
awards.summary: A select community recognition awarded to a small number of OutSystems experts worldwide for technical excellence and community contribution. Sustained since 2022 — backed by 50+ assets published on the OutSystems Forge.

publications.name: Why you should add UUIDs to your database entities
publications.summary: A practical case for surrogate UUIDs over auto-increment integers in enterprise OutSystems data models — and how to do it without paying the storage tax.

publications.name: Automating ODC External Logic Deployments: Smart Versioning and Splitting Workflows in GitHub Actions
publications.summary: How to ship OutSystems Developer Cloud external logic with intelligent versioning, fewer broken builds, and workflows that actually scale across teams.

references.name: Paulo Ribeiro — Senior Technical Writer, OutSystems
references.reference:
António is a very proactive and responsible professional. He has incredible people skills that he continually leverages to contribute to the success of his team.

I had the pleasure of leading António in the role of technical writer for over eight months and was constantly appeased with António's autonomy and critical thinking in delving into the increasingly complex tasks that he tackled over this time.

António left no stone unturned while researching and investigating new subjects. He used a rather systematic approach that involved liaising with the development teams, studying how the competitors tackled similar problems, and always rolling up his sleeves to try things for himself.

This meant that António would build very solid and clear mental models of the subjects at hand, no matter how complex. Very often, António would detect bugs, small loopholes, or even usability weaknesses in the software, that he tirelessly reported back to the development teams.

These traits of António made him an excellent technical writer, especially when producing technical content for a very sophisticated product in a fast-paced company such as OutSystems.

Besides these technical capabilities, António also has very strategic and long-term thinking that helped me guide a rather new team through uncharted territory. Along these past months, I relied on António to provide some historical context and to suggest pragmatic solutions to the impromptu challenges that the team faced.

Last but not least, António's remarkable collaboration skills were vital in helping ramp-up new team members and ensuring that the goals of the team were successfully attained.

Besides being an invaluable asset, António is also a fun and inspiring colleague that any team would be lucky to have.

references.name: Francisco Ferreira — OutSystems Senior Developer, Edge Portugal
references.reference:
It's rare to encounter a talent like António, a person any Dev can count in to manage and help the resolution of problems in the project where I had the pleasure of working alongside him. We worked together close to 8 months in a challenging project with changing deadlines, objectives and a difficult client with preconceived ideas about how a functionality should be done.

I was particularly impressed with António's ability to adapt to the management of an ever-changing project, both in terms of deadlines and tasks and sometimes objectives. I think this is a very important capacity for a good project development since all projects have their problems at some point in time.

António earns by this and other reasons my recommendation as an important member of any development team.

references.name: João Baptista — IT Consultancy, Energy and Environment Engineering
references.reference:
I have worked with António and he is, with no doubts, someone that I can highly recommend to work with.

He has an enormous experience on working with OutSystems platform, once he has a very big know-how on topics, for example, to app architecture, UX/UI, performance, security, delivery, coding best practices and dealed with several projects on different client areas.

António is used to deal with complex business logic and is very efficient to provide solutions for problems and requirements.

António has demonstrated to have very high responsibilities and commitment to his duties on his day-by-day, also great capabilities to lead teams, to distribute tasks, to take decisions, to avoid potential problems, to comply deadlines and to motivate his colleagues to grow up on soft skills and hard skills and is always available to help to them for everything they need.

It was big pleasure to work with António, and I am so grateful to him, once he helped me a lot on my OutSystems journey. Every time I needed, he was able to provide support and passed to me many knowledge bases, hints and tips.

references.name: Dora Ribeiro — Senior Technical Writer, OutSystems (now at European Commission — DG for Digital Services)
references.reference:
António is not what most people think a typical developer is. He's a natural-born networker and communicates with ease. These are great skills when your job entails eliciting information from people and learn new product features and deep insights into their contexts.

Contrary to many engineers he's good at bringing the whole picture into view and never forgets the product's real users might have different needs than the people sitting at R&D.

I think he would be a great asset in a Developer Experience or Relations team or as a Technology Evangelist because of his user focus, social skills and good understanding of the web and mobile development ecosystems, but I'm sure he will be successful in whatever he sets out to do.

references.name: João Fernandes — OutSystems Academy team, OutSystems (now Products at C3 AI)
references.reference:
I helped onboard António into the OutSystems Academy team, and had weekly one-on-ones with him. I worked with him for more than six months.

António has a good technical background. He isn't comfortable documenting something without testing it first. Due to this, he frequently finds bugs in the product that can be fixed before shipping the product to customers.

He also finds ways to improve the UX of the product, helping create a product that is simple to use.

António is also focused on bringing consistency to the documentation. He has created styleguides and worked hard to make the team understand their importance and contribute to his efforts.

António knows how to get his point across either in person or asynchronously (email, IM, ...). He always tries to gather the maximum context available and other points of view, to make informed decisions.

One thing I really like about António, is that he's constantly researching the state of the art, and how other companies are solving a problem our team is trying to solve. This allows the team to make informed decisions, and avoid mistakes others made before.

If given the opportunity I would really enjoy working with António again!

x_talks.title: Lisbon Meetup: OutSystems Agentic Systems Engineering
x_talks.description: A community session on AI-assisted development with OutSystems — new approaches, a hands-on component, and exchange of experiences with the Lisbon OutSystems community.
